title: Logical error: 'Bad cast from type DB::ColumnVector<int> to DB::ColumnTuple'
description:
### Describe the bug
Seems not related to any existing issue. @Avogar it is related to nested data, maybe you can give a look?
### How to reproduce
On a debug build run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Nested(c1 Int, c2 Tuple(c3 Int))) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY tuple();
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (`c0.c1`, `c0.c2`) VALUES ([1], [(1, )]);
ALTER TABLE t0 CLEAR COLUMN `c0.c2`;
OPTIMIZE TABLE t0 FINAL; --Logical error
```
Somehow I cannot reproduce on Fiddle.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c0_c1 INTEGER, c0_c2_c3 INTEGER, c0_c2_c4 TEXT);
INSERT INTO t1 (c0_c1, c0_c2_c3, c0_c2_c4) VALUES (1, 1, 'a');
INSERT INTO t1 (c0_c1, c0_c2_c3, c0_c2_c4) VALUES (2, 2, 'b');
UPDATE t1 SET c0_c2_c3 = NULL WHERE c0_c2_c3 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE c0_c1 IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: ALTER TABLE on nested data with Tuple type
